content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description.txt:1-3 claims "AI-powered suggestions", "built-in ad blocker", and "fully offline" support, but ContentView.swift only implements a plain local array-based to-do list with no AI/ML frameworks (no CoreML/CreateML imports), no ad-blocking/content-blocker extension, and no explicit offline-sync/persistence logic found.
Pierre: Apple's App Review actively checks that marketing claims are backed by real functionality, and unsubstantiated claims like "AI-powered" or "ad blocker" are a common cause of 2.1/2.3 rejections. The only Swift file present is a trivial in-memory task list with no evidence of AI, ad-blocking, or offline persistence code. Either implement these features (or add visible evidence of them, e.g. a CoreML model or content blocker target) or rewrite the description to match what the app actually does.
